title: rosa canina subsp. keissleriana
intro: Rosa canina subsp. keissleriana is a subspecies of the Rosa canina species, commonly known as the wild rose or dog rose, which is a deciduous shrub native to parts of Europe and Western Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly the high content of bioflavonoids and vitamin C in its hips, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as digestive issues and wounds. The health benefits of Rosa canina subsp. keissleriana include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of Rosa canina subsp. keissleriana include teas, tinctures, and infusions made from the hips, which can be used to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
